description: Unit: package metadata satisfies n8n community node naming rules and points to built node entry files.
Unit: build script emits compiled node and credential artifacts without TypeScript compile errors.
Unit: credential definition exposes exactly `baseUrl` and secret `apiKey` fields.
Unit: request helper normalizes base URL with/without trailing slash and avoids double slashes in request paths.
Unit: request helper injects `x-api-key` for every outbound request and never logs raw key values.
Unit: resource selector displays Ticket, Client, Board, Status, and Priority resources in node UI.
Unit: operation selector shows only valid operations for each resource.
Unit: Ticket Create operation builds `POST /api/v1/tickets` payload with required fields mapped correctly.
Unit: Ticket Create operation includes optional fields only when provided.
Integration: Ticket Create returns created ticket body from API `data` wrapper.
Unit: Ticket Get operation sends `GET /api/v1/tickets/{id}` and rejects empty id before request.
Integration: Ticket Get returns expected ticket object from API response.
Unit: Ticket List operation serializes page/limit/sort/order/filter params into `GET /api/v1/tickets` query string.
Integration: Ticket List preserves pagination metadata in node output.
Unit: Ticket Search operation serializes query + optional search filters to `GET /api/v1/tickets/search`.
Integration: Ticket Search returns matching result set and handles empty data array gracefully.
Unit: Ticket Update operation sends `PUT /api/v1/tickets/{id}` with only provided mutable fields.
Integration: Ticket Update returns updated ticket object for downstream nodes.
Unit: Ticket Update Status operation sends `PUT /api/v1/tickets/{id}/status` with `status_id` payload.
Unit: Ticket Update Assignment operation sends `PUT /api/v1/tickets/{id}/assignment` with `assigned_to` payload.
Unit: Ticket Delete operation sends `DELETE /api/v1/tickets/{id}`.
Integration: Ticket Delete converts HTTP 204 response to `{ success: true, id, deleted: true }` output.
Unit: response normalizer unwraps `{ data: ... }` for single-object responses.
Unit: response normalizer unwraps `{ data: [...] }` and includes pagination when provided.
Unit: 401 API response is surfaced as invalid/missing API key with API details in error output.
Unit: 403 API response is surfaced as permission error with API details in error output.
Unit: 404 API response is surfaced as not-found error with API details in error output.
Unit: 400 API response is surfaced as validation/reference error with API details in error output.
Integration: Continue On Fail mode emits item-level error objects and continues processing remaining items.
Unit: Client helper operation maps to `GET /api/v1/clients` and returns list output.
Unit: Board helper operation maps to `GET /api/v1/boards` and returns list output.
Unit: Status helper operation maps to `GET /api/v1/statuses` and returns list output.
Unit: Priority helper operation maps to `GET /api/v1/priorities` and returns list output.
Integration: load-options for `client_id` returns label/value pairs from clients API.
Integration: load-options for `board_id` returns label/value pairs from boards API.
Integration: load-options for `status_id` returns label/value pairs from statuses API.
Integration: load-options for `priority_id` returns label/value pairs from priorities API.
Unit: when load-options call fails, required ID fields remain manually editable and operation can still execute.
Unit: ticket create/update UI groups required fields separately from optional additional fields.
Unit: operation validation blocks outbound call when required ticket IDs or search query are missing.
Docs: README includes credential setup, operation matrix, and ticket field requirements.
Docs: installation section covers self-hosted npm/manual install paths and unverified-node cloud limitation.
Docs: example workflows include one create->update-assignment flow and one search->update-status flow.
Release: initial npm publish produces installable package version and release notes reflecting supported operations.
